---
title: "Imke Tomsin" 
subtitle: "PhD student - FWO fundamental research fellow"
page-layout: full
image: images/Imke.jpg # Use same image as the card on people.qmd
about:
  template: trestles # Places photo/links in a neat left sidebar
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:imke.tomsin@uhasselt.be # maybe add option to hover and show mailadress?
    - icon: linkedin ## Change icons!!
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/imke-tomsin-740107122/
    - text: "{{< fa brands orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0009-4894-1487
    - text: "{{< fa brands researchgate >}} ResearchGate"
      href: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Imke-Tomsin-3?ev=hdr_xprf
---

## About Me

My research focuses on the development and application of mechanistic models to support decision-making in wildlife management and conservation. I am particularly interested in human-wildlife interactions within social-ecological systems, with a focus on mammals including roe deer, wild boar, and wolves, and previously the European hamster.


---

## Research Interests

* **Individual-/agent-based models**
* **(Generalised) Management Strategy Evaluation**
* **Ungulate species and their predators** 
* **Good Modelling Practices**
* **Human-wildlife conflict**

---

## Publications

### 2025

* Tomsin, I., Duthie, A. B., Bunnefeld, N., Leirs, H., Casaer, J., & Beenaerts, N. (2025). Evaluating Management Scenarios for the European Hamster (Cricetus cricetus) Using Quantitative Models. Ecology and Evolution, 15(10), e72353. https://doi.org/10.1002/ece3.72353

## Other Outreach 

### Articles 

* Natuurfocus 

### Presentations

* Benelux zoology conference
* International Wildlife Conference 2025

---

## Collaborators & Partners

::: {.grid .align-items-center .g-4 .my-3}

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-3 .text-center}
[![](images/logos/FWO.png){.collaborator-logo alt="FWO"}](https://www.fwo.be)
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-3 .text-center}
[![](images/logos/CMK.png){.collaborator-logo alt="Hasselt University"}](https://www.uhasselt.be)
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-3 .text-center}
[![](images/logos/INBO.png){.collaborator-logo alt="INBO"}](https://www.vlaanderen.be/inbo)
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-3 .text-center}
[![](images/logos/Stirling.jpg){.collaborator-logo alt="University of Stirling"}]
:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-md-3 .text-center}
[![](images/logos/Antwerpen.png){.collaborator-logo alt="University of Antwerp"}](https://www.uantwerpen.be)
:::

:::

* Hasselt university: main institute - Centre for Environmental Science
* FWO: funder - PhD fellowship fundamental research 
* INBO: co-supervisor Dr. ir. Jim Casaer
* University of Stirling: co-supervisors Prof. Dr. Nils Bunnefeld, Dr. Brad Duthie
* University of Antwerp: co-supervisor Prof. Dr. Herwig Leirs
*
